architecture_fit.note: Minimal delta: single attention layer, no MLP, W_Q=W_K=identity over one-hot embedding; directly produces token-match attention reaching 0.94 alignment.

baseline_comparison.note: Beats the uniform baseline (0.125) by a clear lift and the temperature-collapse strawman shows alignment falling to ~baseline, though the only baseline is the framework's trivial uniform attention.

faithfulness.note: Honestly labeled hand-built; the temperature knob is a genuine causal manipulation of the matching logits, but it is a self-ablation of the synthetic circuit and no real-model patch/ablation is proposed.

operating_range.note: Only the canonical seq_len=8 / vocab=50 condition; varies across swap/rotation/random structure but no sweep over input scale or sequence length across orders of magnitude.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set (identity QK over one-hot tokens with a temperature) — the explicit point of the attempt, reproducing target behaviour without learning.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab leads with alignment-vs-baseline bars plus per-position lines and an interactive temperature heatmap with red true-source boxes — makes the claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README explicitly ties each panel to the benchmarked quantity, justifies the baseline line and per-position grain, and frames the temperature heatmap as the causal knob.

notes: A clean, honest hand-built circuit that solves the anagram-alignment task with a single MLP-free attention head, hitting 0.94 alignment vs 0.125 uniform baseline with strong robustness across permutation types. Architecture fit, hardcoded-weights bonus, and visualisation rationale are excellent; the temperature slider gives a credible causal demonstration. Weakest on operating range (no input-scale sweep) and on faithfulness to an actual trained model, since the circuit is synthetic.
